text:
BOTTOM LINE — AI melt-up, defensives dumped, gold ignored.  
Semis and mega-cap tech ripped on upside AI earnings surprises from Dell and Snowflake and an Asian chip-cycle bid, forcing performance chasers into growth while quality and momentum regimes stayed firmly bullish and value leadership persisted. Flows rotated out of Energy and Utilities as oil collapsed on U.S.-Iran ceasefire optimism and weak consumer confidence and capex weighed on Staples and Industrials despite index-level record highs.

INDICES — VIX 16.4, grinding lower toward the 52-week floor.  
SOXX +8.61% > QQQ +4.23% > SPY +2.44% > DIA +0.90% > IWM/IJH +0.00%, with LARGE > SMALL by 3.62pp as growth and semis dominated tape. The standout cross-asset move was GLD -1.41% against TLT +1.09%, a clean “real-yields up, gold down” transmission.

VOLUME — RSP 1.7x and SPY 1.2x rising with prices, classic broad-market accumulation, while QQQ volume was roughly average but still paired with a 4.23% gain, showing strong but not blowout conviction in growth. XLRE 1.6x volume on a -2.67% day and XLY 1.2x on +2.54% flag discrete distribution in real estate and accumulation in discretionary, while GLD 0.6x and SLV 0.7x on down moves show metals being sold quietly rather than in panic.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +5.20%, XLY Discretionary +2.54%, XLI Industrials +2.22%, XLC Comms +0.89%, XLF Financials +0.07%, XLV Healthcare -1.05%, XLE Energy -1.36%, XLB Materials -1.49%, XLU Utilities -1.86%, XLP Staples -1.94%, XLRE Real Estate -2.67%.  
Top industries: SOXX +8.61%, MSOS +8.32%, SMH +7.24%, CIBR +5.26%; bottom: VNQ -2.27%, UTES -3.03%, IHI -3.17%, WGMI -4.05%. Money is clearly rotating into AI semis and broader tech plus select cyclicals, while real estate, utilities, energy, and defensive staples are funding sources. Overall rotation-state label: broad risk-on.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 0.85 with skew +5.3% and VIX below its 20-day MA shows moderate protection demand but no stress, consistent with a trending tape rather than panic. QQQ PCR_vol 1.27 and skew +12.6% with price well above the 729.0 gamma flip mean upside is being chased with puts still rich, so dealer hedging should dampen intraday swings unless spot reverses hard.

CROSS-ASSET — Gold -1.41% with GLD under pressure while long Treasuries via TLT +1.09% and the dollar UUP -0.07% say the move is more about real-rate repricing than classic risk-off. Oil’s recent -17.55% 20d slide in USO is transmitting directly into XLE -1.36% and high-volume selling in OIH and FCG, as ceasefire optimism drives down the crude risk premium.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63, firmly Greed, with KLAC +21.34% in The Fab 7, PWR +4.68% in The Buildout 7, and MLM -6.39% in The Housing 7 as top tape movers. XLK is the best 20d sector on a strong AI infrastructure impulse while GLD is the worst, and SOXX is the best basket with WGMI the worst, underscoring a clean “chips over miners” momentum extreme. KLAC’s 2.4x vol spike with price up is clear accumulation tied to AI-exposed chip-cap news, while CVX’s 2.1x vol spike with price down -3.10% shows energy distribution tied to the Iran ceasefire/oil shock narrative. Watch PWR (+4.68%) for continued data-center buildout flow, IJT (+1.30%) for emerging small-cap growth leadership with a 3.1x vol spike, and GLD (-1.41%) for potential further downside if real yields keep grinding higher and options skew stays bid.
